name: Crispy Chicken with Lemon Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the chicken in simmering chicken stock or water until nearly tender, about 20 minutes.
2. Remove and suspend the chicken over a bowl to dry at room temperature for at least 4 hours, or until thoroughly cooled.
3. Heat enough oil to 350°F in a wok for deep-frying whole chicken.
4. Add the chicken and fry for 20 minutes, turning until golden on all sides.
5. Meanwhile, combine lemon juice with the squeezed lemon half, sugar, and water in a saucepan. Bring to boil and simmer for 2 to 3 minutes until sugar is dissolved.
6. Add 2 teaspoons of oil and soy sauce. Remove from heat and set aside.
7. Split the chicken into halves along the back and cut into serving pieces.
8. Reassemble on a serving platter and garnish with lemon peel triangles, if desired.
9. Bring the reserved lemon sauce to boil again.
10. Mix cornstarch with a small amount of cold water to make a paste and stir into the lemon sauce. Cook, stirring until slightly thickened.
11. Spoon over chicken pieces.
